design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one combat with dual health bars; player defeats opponent by exhausting both health bars rather than winning best-of-three rounds. Loop emphasizes sustained offensive pressure and combo execution.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Maintaining combo offense while remaining vulnerable to combo breakers; managing two health bars creates higher stakes than traditional fighting games.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Execute spectacular multi-hit combos with flashy finishing moves; control diverse, visually distinctive fighters (cyborg, alien, skeleton, velociraptor, etc.) in high-speed combat.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Dual health bar system removes health recovery between rounds, fundamentally changing risk/reward of aggressive play. Auto-doubles and combo breakers democratize combos while maintaining execution skill gap. Features finishing moves (No Mercy, Ultra Combo, Humiliation) as core reward fantasy rather than optional flourish.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Tournament mode and training mode available, suggesting adjustable AI difficulty and practice tools for skill development.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Single-player tournament ladder; defeat opponents to progress toward final boss Eyedol.
replayability_hooks: 10 playable characters with distinct movesets encourage character mastery
Combo breaker system creates defensive play counter-strategies
Finishing move execution (No Mercy, Ultra Combo, Humiliation) provides stylistic variation

world_building_techniques: Character select descriptions establish fighter backgrounds (Native American chief, escaped convict, alien abductee, undead warrior, etc.)
Tournament premise: Ultratech megacorporation organizes Killer Instinct tournament to test experimental creatures
Dimensional plot device: Eyedol released from Limbo dimensional prison by Ultratech's interdimensional bridge technology

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic final opponent with experimental nature; Eyedol is two-headed cyclops/satyr hybrid created by Ultratech's dimensional breach technology, representing pinnacle of corporation's power.
notable_bosses: Eyedol (final boss): Two-headed cyclops creature from dimensional prison

faction_indicators: Chief Thunder: Native American seeking brother (family loyalty)
Ultratech creations: Fulgore (cyborg enforcer), Glacius (alien), Riptor (velociraptor clone), Cinder (chemical accident victim)
danger_signals: Ultratech megacorporation: controlling, experimental, dimensional-breaching technology
Forced combatants: Glacius abducted, Riptor cloned, Cinder chemically transformed
Eyedol release: reckless dimensional experimentation with unstable consequences
environmental_storytelling: Character roster diversity reflects Ultratech's reach: human fighters, alien subjects, genetic experiments, supernatural beings
Tournament structure masks corporate experimentation agenda
Fighting arenas host creatures from different dimensions/origins
